stories.title: 'Only Human: Bound' audio drama explores artificial intelligence
stories.simple_summary: A radio drama called 'Only Human: Bound' explores what artificial intelligence is and how it affects people.
stories.why_it_matters: This is entertainment — it doesn't affect your daily life or business, though it might be interesting to listen to if you want to understand AI better.

stories.title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
stories.simple_summary: A company called Anthropic created a new tool called Claude Science to help scientists develop medicines by organizing all their research data and information in one place.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mainly to pharmaceutical companies and research laboratories — unless you work in healthcare or medicine, this doesn't directly affect you or a small business.

stories.title: Mobile Artificial Intelligence Market Size to Surpass USD 322.21 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
stories.simple_summary: The AI market for smartphones and mobile devices is expected to become very large and worth hundreds of billions of dollars by 2035.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — nothing you need to act on right now.

stories.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
stories.simple_summary: A new museum in Boston has an AI chatbot that talks to you like Alexander Hamilton, a famous Founding Father, and teaches about money and finance.
stories.why_it_matters: This is entertainment at a museum — it's fun but doesn't affect your business or finances.

stories.title: AI prey: why watchdogs are telling parents to protect children from nudification apps
stories.simple_summary: Parents need to watch out for apps that use AI to remove clothes from photos of children — it's a dangerous tool being misused.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have children or grandchildren, you should know about this risk and talk to them about staying safe online.

stories.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
stories.simple_summary: A company called Midjourney showed a video of a futuristic machine that could do medical ultrasounds cheaply, but they haven't proven yet that it actually works well.
stories.why_it_matters: This is an interesting future idea, but right now there's no proof it works — it's too early to rely on or worry about.

stories.title: Mom is an artificial intelligence pioneer. Her kids couldn’t disagree more about AI.
stories.simple_summary: One woman is a pioneer in AI technology, but her own children have very different opinions about whether AI is good or bad.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a human interest story — it shows that even experts disagree about AI, so it's normal to have questions.

stories.title: The Enterprise Gives AI Models a Path to Consumer Loyalty
stories.simple_summary: A company is using AI to help keep customers loyal and coming back to buy more.
stories.why_it_matters: Large businesses are experimenting with AI to understand customers better — if you own a small business, this is something to watch but not something you need to jump into right now.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
stories.simple_summary: Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta (which owns Facebook), said that progress on AI is moving slower than he expected.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech workers — it shows that even big companies sometimes overestimate how fast AI will advance.

stories.title: Artificial intelligence: Yann LeCun works on more flexible AI
stories.simple_summary: A famous AI researcher named Yann LeCun is working on making AI systems more flexible and able to handle different kinds of tasks.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a technical research project — it doesn't affect you or your business directly.

stories.title: Read the Tense Emails Between the Pentagon (Former Uber Exec) and Anthropic (Dario Amodei)
stories.simple_summary: There were tense email conversations between the Pentagon and a company called Anthropic about AI and military use.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a story for people interested in government and technology policy — it doesn't affect ordinary people or small businesses.

stories.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
stories.simple_summary: Jersey Mike's, a sandwich restaurant chain, mentioned AI in their public documents even though a sandwich shop doesn't really need AI, showing that companies are using the word 'AI' just to sound modern.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a reminder to be skeptical when companies claim to use AI — sometimes it's just marketing hype and not a real benefit.
